probe_rows.label: surface_shell_bending_rocm_residual_replay
probe_rows.claim_boundary: ROCm sparse CSR matvec residual replay using a CPU reference solution. This proves device residency and sparse residual parity for the assembled matrix, but it is not a GPU sparse solver closure.

probe_rows.label: surface_shell_bending_rocm_sparse_solve_attempt

matrix_diagnostics.claim_boundary: Matrix structure diagnostics for selecting a GPU sparse-solve strategy. A large connected component means frame-style component dense direct solves are not a feasible closure path.

rocm_sparse_symmetric_scaled_bicgstab.claim_boundary: Symmetric diagonal scaling probe: solves (D^-1/2 A D^-1/2)y=D^-1/2 b on ROCm and reports the true original-system residual for x=D^-1/2 y. It is only solver closure if that true residual meets the requested tolerance.

rocm_sparse_host_ilu_device_gmres.claim_boundary: Host ILU factorization and preconditioner apply with ROCm torch sparse matvec GMRES. Closure is granted only when the returned solution passes the full assembled CSR residual replay in Python. This is not a CPU sparse-direct fallback solve.

candidate_rows.claim_boundary: Host ILU factorization and preconditioner apply with ROCm torch sparse matvec GMRES. Closure is granted only when the returned solution passes the full assembled CSR residual replay in Python. This is not a CPU sparse-direct fallback solve.

claim_boundary: This is a ROCm sparse backend probe for assembled MGT line/frame/shell/coupled matrices. It proves the full 3-DOF line sparse matrix can run on AMD ROCm/PyTorch sparse CG and the full 6-DOF frame elastic matrix can run as component-wise ROCm dense direct solves. It also replays full shell and coupled frame-shell sparse residuals on ROCm CSR tensors using CPU reference solutions and records actual ROCm sparse solve attempts for those full matrices. Shell/coupled sparse solve attempts remain unclosed when PyTorch ROCm sparse-direct support is unavailable or iterative preconditioners plus defect-correction refinement fail to converge.
